text.title: Build-in-Public Engine Output Contract
text.summary: Defines what "engine running" looks like as a weekly artifact set and what a healthy 1/3/6-month engine produces in metric floor terms.

list.item: weekly-input-log/yyyy-Www.md: four-field log committed by Sunday of the following week.
posts/yyyy-Www/: published-post sources, one file per post, with hook line on first line.
repurposing/yyyy-Www.md: chain status — X thread, LinkedIn version, blog draft, newsletter snippet (date and URL per platform).
1 longer thread per week (format 1 or 2 expanded).
1 repurposing pass per longer thread within 7 days.
Weekly metric snapshot in metrics/yyyy-Www.csv: net-new followers, substantive replies, list signups.

section.title: Metric floor targets

list.item: Month 1: cadence holds; metrics tracked; no growth assumed.
Month 3: net-new followers/week trending positive; first 5+ substantive inbound DMs per month; first 10+ newsletter signups directly attributable to the engine.
Month 6: 50+ newsletter signups/month from engine; at least 1 inbound business opportunity (interview, beta sign, customer call) per month attributed.

section.p: Targets are floors, not vanity stretch. Missing month 3 floor for 2 consecutive months is a signal to revisit r2/r3/r4, not to abandon the engine.

antipattern.description: Posting from generic LLM prompts without referencing the weekly-input-log entry.
antipattern.reason: Produces format-shaped slop indistinguishable from spam accounts. Burns audience trust.

antipattern.description: Tracking only likes / impressions in metrics/.
antipattern.reason: r5 violation. Optimizing for likes optimizes for non-business outcomes.

antipattern.description: Repurposing chain ends at LinkedIn — no email list capture.
antipattern.reason: X and LinkedIn are rented audiences. Without an email endpoint, the engine builds zero owned distribution.

list.item: All posts trace to a log entry (or are explicitly tagged "ad-hoc" and counted separately).
Longer thread has a repurposing row with at least one downstream URL within 7 days.
Metrics snapshot includes the three non-vanity numbers, not just follower count.
